summary: Matched the common Bowser capture action file `ftCo_CaptureKoopa` at 100%, replacing a block of hand-written assembly in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with a new C translation and a dedicated header. The PR also added motion-variable layout for `capturekoopa`, factored shared capture setup through an inline helper, exposed needed JObj translation helpers in `jobj.h`, and preserved remaining small-data strings via asm sidecar files for `ftCo_0B3E` and `ftCo_YoshiEgg`.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKoopa.c` implementing `ftCo_800BC438`, `ftCo_800BC458`, `ftCo_800BC4A8`, `ftCo_800BC7E0`, `ftCo_800BC8D4`, and the empty ground/air CaptureKoopa Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks.
Removed the corresponding `ftCo_800BC438` through `ftCo_CaptureKoopaAir_Coll` assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; the PR report shows the new C file and all listed functions at 100.00%.
Added `ftCo_CaptureKoopa.h` with typed prototypes and moved CaptureKoopa declarations out of the broad `ft_08A4.h` header.
Added `capturekoopa` fields to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` at fighter offsets `fp+2340` through `fp+2350`, including a bool at `x0`, an unknown `x4`, and floats `x8`, `xC`, and `x10`.

Added JObj inline helpers `HSD_JObjGetTranslationZ`, `HSD_JObjAddTranslationY`, and `HSD_JObjAddTranslationZ` to `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h`, including the same HSD assert line numbers and dirty-matrix behavior needed by the matched code.

Created asm sidecar files `asm/melee/ft/ftCo_0B3E.s` and `asm/melee/ft/ftCo_YoshiEgg.s` for `.sdata` strings such as `"jobj.h"`, `"jobj"`, and `"scale"`, while deleting those labels from the monolithic `ft_08A4.s` sdata block.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to build the new C object and the new asm sidecar objects in the fighter object list.
Added includes for `ftCo_CaptureKoopa.h` in related common/Bowser files so typed prototypes are available where the capture action is referenced.
smart_moves: Used a static inline helper, `inlineA0`, to express the nearly identical setup sequence for `ftCo_800BC7E0` and `ftCo_800BC8D4`; the two public functions differ only by the callback passed in (`ftCo_800BC9C8` versus `ftCo_800BCAF4`).

Matched JObj translation mutations through small inline wrappers in `jobj.h` rather than open-coding field access everywhere; this preserved assert behavior and the conditional `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty` call when `JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT` is not set.

Kept unrelated or still-unmatched small-data labels in assembly sidecar files instead of forcing them into the new C file, reducing risk to section ordering while still allowing the text functions to move to C.
Converted raw `fp+2340` style accesses into a named `mv.co.capturekoopa` motion-variable struct, making the matched C more reusable for later common fighter capture work.
Added focused headers/includes so declarations live near the decompiled action file instead of leaving more `UNK_RET` declarations in `ft_08A4.h`.
decomp_lessons: For action files with shared ground/air setup, try an inline helper taking the one differing callback; this can match duplicate assembly sequences while keeping C readable.

When decompiling code that calls HSD inline APIs, matching may depend on reproducing assert line numbers and dirty-flag side effects in the header-level inline, not just matching the high-level field operation.
Motion-variable unions are important for replacing raw offsets: in this PR, offsets `2340`, `2348`, `234C`, and `2350` became `mv.co.capturekoopa.x0`, `x8`, `xC`, and `x10`.
If extracting a C file disturbs `.sdata` ownership or order, split residual string constants into small asm files tied to the appropriate module and add those objects explicitly to `obj_files.mk`.
Empty callbacks for Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll should still be emitted as explicit empty functions when the original assembly was just `blr`; these count toward full action-state matching.
naming_conventions: New common action file and header follow the `ftCo_CaptureKoopa.c` / `ftCo_CaptureKoopa.h` pattern.
Action callbacks use the common fighter naming pattern `ftCo_CaptureKoopa_Anim`, `ftCo_CaptureKoopa_IASA`, `ftCo_CaptureKoopa_Phys`, `ftCo_CaptureKoopa_Coll`, plus `CaptureKoopaAir` variants.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as `ftCo_800BC4A8`, while now receiving typed parameters instead of `UNK_PARAMS`.
Motion variables are grouped under `mv.co.capturekoopa`, with provisional field names `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, and `x10` where semantic names were not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed 457 lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` and replaced them with 103 lines of C plus targeted sdata asm sidecars.
Used `ABS(fp_x1A50 + HSD_JObjGetTranslationZ(jobj)) <= fp->mv.co.capturekoopa.x10` and the analogous Y comparison to match the branch/control flow around joint translation bounds.

Used JObj add-translation inlines to reproduce the assembly pattern that updates `translate.y` or `translate.z`, checks JObj flags, and calls `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub` through the existing dirty helper.
Preserved float constants originally in `.sdata2` implicitly through the C expressions using `0`, `1`, and integer-to-float calculations rather than keeping those constants in `ft_08A4.s`.
Kept `.sdata` strings such as `ftCo_804D3B68`, `ftCo_804D3B70`, `ftCo_804D3B74`, `ftCo_804D3B7C`, `ftCo_804D3B88`, `ftCo_804D3B90`, and `ftCo_804D3B98` in asm with explicit `.balign` directives.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/report indicates a complete match: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKoopa.c` scored 100.00%, with every listed function also at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `mv.co.capturekoopa.x0`, `x8`, `xC`, or `x10` to semantic names.
Search for additional uses of `HSD_JObjAddTranslationY` and `HSD_JObjAddTranslationZ` after this PR to see whether the inline helpers became a reusable matching tactic.
Compare future `ftCo_Capture*` matches for the same `inlineA0` setup pattern around victim assignment, facing direction, `ftCommon_8007DBCC`, and `ftCo_800DB368`.
Look for follow-up work that removes the asm sidecar strings from `ftCo_0B3E.s` or `ftCo_YoshiEgg.s` once their owning C files are fully decompiled.
